q: What are ACI Service Rules?
a: ACI Service Rules are the company’s employment guidelines covering employment terms, salary, benefits, leave, transfer, separation, conduct and other service-related matters.

q: Who is covered under the Service Rules?
a: The Service Rules generally apply to eligible management employees of ACI, unless a separate business-specific policy or employee-category policy applies.

q: Who should I contact for Service Rule clarification?
a: Employees should contact the People Team for any clarification, interpretation or support regarding Service Rules.

q: Can Service Rules be changed?
a: Yes, Service Rules may be amended where necessary in line with applicable Labour Law/Rules and company requirements. Final authority rests with the Management Committee.

q: Can employees suggest changes to Service Rules?
a: Employees may share suggestions through proper channel to the People Team. Any formal amendment will require review and approval by the appropriate authority.

q: What happens if any rule conflicts with Labour Law?
a: Applicable Labour Law/Rules will prevail where legally required, and the company may amend the rule accordingly through the appropriate authority.

q: Are all Service Rules applicable to every employee?
a: Not always. Some rules may vary based on employee category, grade, business, location, appointment terms or separate policy.

q: What should I do if I am unsure about a policy?
a: Employees should not assume. They should contact the People Team before taking any action involving benefits, leave, travel, separation or disciplinary matters.

q: Is exception allowed under Service Rules?
a: Exceptions may be considered only through proper approval from the authorized authority, depending on the nature of the case.

q: How many management grades does ACI currently have?
a: ACI currently follows 10 management grades, from OM01 to OM10, along with other business- or job category-specific grade structures where applicable.

q: Why are employees placed in different grades?
a: Grades are based on role level, responsibility, accountability, business need and company-approved structure.

q: Can grade structure change?
a: Yes, grade structure may be reviewed or changed by Management based on organizational need and approved policy.

q: What is the difference between probation and confirmation?
a: Probation is the initial assessment period. Confirmation means the employee has successfully completed the probation period and has been formally confirmed by the company.

q: Is confirmation automatic after probation period?
a: No. Confirmation depends on performance, conduct, role suitability and approval by the authorized authority.

q: Who confirms an employee?
a: Confirmation is processed through the proper approval channel, usually involving the reporting manager, Business/Function Head and People Team.

q: What happens if performance during probation is not satisfactory?
a: If performance during probation is not satisfactory, the company may either separate/terminate the employment as per appointment terms and applicable policy, or place the employee under a Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) after completion of 90 days, depending on the case and management decision.

q: When is salary usually paid?
a: Salary is usually paid monthly through the employee’s nominated bank account, as per payroll schedule.

q: What happens if someone joins in the middle of a month?
a: Salary is paid on a pro-rata basis from the actual joining date.

q: Are salary details confidential?
a: Yes. Salary and benefit information are confidential and should not be disclosed casually or without authorization.

q: Can I request a salary advance?
a: Salary advance is generally discouraged and may be considered only in exceptional cases with proper approval.

q: Who should I contact for salary-related issues?
a: Employees should contact the People Team or payroll-support authority through the official process.

q: When is annual increment considered?
a: Annual increment is normally considered as part of the annual performance and compensation review process.

q: Who is eligible for annual increment?
a: To be eligible for increment in that financial year, the employee needs to be confirmed on or before 01 April of that financial year, subject to performance and approval.

q: Is annual increment guaranteed?
a: No. Annual increment is not automatic. It depends on confirmation status, performance, company affordability and management approval.

q: Can an employee receive special increment?
a: Special increment may be considered only in exceptional cases based on strong justification and approval by the authorized authority.

q: Can a newly confirmed employee receive annual increment in the same year?
a: Yes, only if the employee is confirmed on or before 01 April of that financial year and meets other applicable criteria.

q: Who is eligible for festival bonus?
a: Eligible confirmed employees may receive festival bonus as per company policy.

q: How many festival bonuses are usually paid?
a: Two festival bonuses are generally paid in a year, subject to eligibility and applicable policy.

q: What is the usual amount of festival bonus?
a: Festival bonus is generally equivalent to one basic salary for each eligible festival bonus, subject to applicable policy.

q: When is festival bonus paid?
a: Festival bonus is usually paid before the applicable festival, as per company payroll schedule.

q: Is festival bonus applicable to all religions?
a: One bonus is paid during Eid-ul-Fitr and the other during the major religious festival of the employee’s respective religion.

q: Who is eligible for performance bonus?
a: Employees confirmed on or before 01 April of the financial year may be eligible for performance bonus, subject to performance rating, policy and approval.

q: Is performance bonus guaranteed?
a: No. Performance bonus depends on eligibility, performance outcome, appraisal completion, business result and management approval.

q: When is performance bonus paid?
a: Performance bonus is paid between 1 September and 30 September, subject to completion of the appraisal cycle and approval.

q: Do Field Sales employees receive performance bonus?
a: Field Sales employees may follow separate sales incentive or business-specific reward policy instead of the corporate performance bonus structure.

q: Who approves performance bonus?
a: Performance bonus is finalized through the approved performance management and management approval process.

q: What types of leave are available?
a: Leave may include Privilege Leave, Sick Leave, Maternity Leave and other approved leave types as per company policy and applicable law.

q: Is leave an employee right or subject to approval?
a: Leave is an employee benefit, but availing leave requires proper application and approval based on business need and policy.

q: How should I apply for leave?
a: Leave should be applied through the official leave process or system, with approval from the reporting manager or authorized authority.

q: Can leave be taken without approval?
a: No. Leave should be approved in advance unless there is an emergency or illness.

q: What should I do in case of sudden illness?
a: Inform the reporting manager as early as possible and complete the official leave process as soon as practical. Medical documents may be required.

q: Can unused leave be carried forward?
a: Leave carry-forward depends on the applicable company leave policy. Employees should check with the People Team for their current entitlement.

q: Can leave be encashed?
a: Leave encashment, if applicable, will follow the approved company policy and separation/encashment rules.

q: Can leave be rejected?
a: Yes. Leave may be declined or rescheduled based on business urgency, manpower requirement or policy limitations.

q: Is maternity leave provided?
a: Maternity leave is provided as per applicable Labour Law/Rules and company policy.

q: Can an employee remain absent without approval?
a: No. Unauthorized absence may be treated as misconduct and may lead to disciplinary action.

q: Is attendance recording mandatory?
a: Yes. Employees must follow the company’s attendance recording process where attendance recording system is applicable.

q: What happens if I forget to punch attendance?
a: The employee should immediately inform the reporting manager/authorized person and regularize it through the official process. Habitual non-compliance may lead to action.

q: What happens if an employee is habitually late?
a: Habitual late attendance may be treated as violation of company discipline and may lead to disciplinary action.

q: Are office timings the same for all locations?
a: Office timing may vary by location, business, function or operational requirement. Employees should follow the timing applicable to their office or workplace.

q: What are the current head office locations?
a: Current head office locations include ACI Centre, Novo Tower, Simpletree Anarkoli, Police Plaza, Shanta Forum, Trade Intercontinental, Aristo Tower and Edison Prime.

q: Which TA/DA policy applies to corporate employees?
a: Corporate/management employees follow the TA/DA Policy for Corporate Employees, revised on 01 August 2025.

q: Does the same TA/DA policy apply to Field Sales employees?
a: No. Field Sales employees follow separate TA/DA policies, which may vary from business to business.

q: What are the TA/DA entitlements for corporate employees?
a: Entitlements depend on grade: OM01–OM03: Meal 800, Accommodation up to 2,500 or 3,000 for special cities, DA 350, TA 1st Class A/C Bus/Train/Steamer, Stay with Relative 600. OM04–OM07: Meal 900, Accommodation up to 3,000 or 3,500 for special cities, DA 450, TA 1st Class A/C Bus/Train/Steamer, Stay with Relative 750. OM08–OM09: Meal and Accommodation at actual, DA 650, TA 1st Class AC Bus/Train/Steamer/Air, Stay with Relative 1,000. OM10 & Above: Meal and Accommodation at actual, DA 650, TA 1st Class AC Bus/Train/Steamer/Air, Stay with Relative 1,100.

q: Which cities are considered special cities under TA/DA?
a: Special cities are Dhaka, Chattogram, Sylhet, Barishal, Cox’s Bazar and Khulna.

q: Am I eligible for accommodation and DA if I stay with relatives or friends?
a: No. If an employee stays with friends or relatives during travel, the employee is not eligible for Accommodation and DA. The “Stay with Relative” amount may apply as per grade.

q: Is DA payable for same-day return from outstation?
a: No. Same-day return from outstation is not eligible for Daily Allowance.

q: Can I rent a car during official travel?
a: Car rental expenses must be pre-approved by the respective Head of Department.

q: Who approves exceptions under TA/DA policy?
a: Any exception must be approved by the authorized approving authority.

q: Can ACI transfer an employee?
a: Yes. The company may transfer employees based on business need, subject to applicable policy and approval.

q: Will transfer-related expenses be reimbursed?
a: Eligible transfer-related expenses may be reimbursed as per approved transfer policy and entitlement.

q: Is notice provided before transfer?
a: Transfer notice depends on business need and policy. Employees should follow the official transfer instruction issued by the company.

q: Can an employee refuse transfer?
a: Employees are expected to comply with official transfer instructions unless there is a valid reason accepted by Management.

q: Who should I contact for transfer support?
a: Employees should contact the People Team and relevant Administration/Finance support teams for transfer-related processing.

q: Do employees receive medical allowance?
a: Eligible employees receive medical allowance as part of salary structure or as per applicable policy.

q: Is hospitalization support available?
a: Hospitalization support may be available for eligible employees and dependents as per company policy.

q: Is prior approval required for hospitalization claim?
a: Prior approval may be required depending on the type of treatment and policy condition. Employees should contact the People Team before claim submission where possible.

q: Are all medical expenses reimbursable?
a: No. Only eligible medical expenses are reimbursable as per company policy. Non-eligible or excluded expenses will not be reimbursed.

q: Are cosmetic treatments reimbursable?
a: Cosmetic or beautification-related treatments are generally not reimbursable unless specifically approved under policy.

q: Are dependents covered under medical benefit?
a: Dependent coverage, if applicable, will follow the company’s medical benefit policy and eligibility rules.

q: Who verifies medical claims?
a: Medical claims are reviewed through the authorized approval and verification process involving relevant People Team/Finance/authorized authority.

q: Who is eligible for gratuity?
a: Gratuity is applicable after completion of 01 year of service, subject to company gratuity rules.

q: How is gratuity calculated?
a: Gratuity is generally calculated based on last drawn basic salary and eligible completed service period, as per the approved gratuity rule.

q: How is partial service period treated for gratuity?
a: As per latest instruction, after completion of 1 year, if an employee completes an additional 6 months, that period may be treated as a full year for gratuity calculation, subject to final policy wording.

q: What is the gratuity rate for service up to 10 years?
a: For eligible service, gratuity is generally calculated at 30 days / 1 month basic salary for each eligible year, subject to company rule.

q: What is the gratuity rate for service above 10 years?
a: For more than 10 years of eligible service, gratuity is generally calculated at 45 days / 1.5 months basic salary for each eligible year, subject to company rule.

q: Is gratuity paid automatically during service?
a: No. Gratuity is normally payable during separation/retirement/death-related settlement, subject to eligibility and clearance.

q: When can an employee join Provident Fund?
a: An employee can join Provident Fund after completion of 01 year of continued service.

q: What is the employee PF contribution rate?
a: The employee may contribute 8% of basic salary to Provident Fund.

q: Does the company contribute to PF?
a: Yes. The company contributes the same amount after completion of 01 year of contribution, subject to Provident Fund rules.

q: Can I withdraw PF while in service?
a: Withdrawal or loan from PF, if allowed, will follow the approved Provident Fund rules.

q: Who manages Provident Fund rules?
a: Provident Fund is governed by the approved PF rules and relevant trustees/authorized authority.

q: Is Group Life Insurance available?
a: Group Life Insurance may be available for eligible employees as per company policy and insurance terms.

q: Who receives insurance benefit in case of death?
a: Insurance benefit is payable to the nominated person/legal beneficiary as per insurance and company records.

q: Should employees update nominee information?
a: Yes. Employees should keep nominee information updated for PF, gratuity, insurance and other relevant benefits.

q: What are common types of separation?
a: Separation may include resignation, retirement, termination, dismissal, redundancy, death or other legally recognized forms of separation.

q: How should an employee resign?
a: An employee should submit resignation through proper channel, following the required notice period and approval process.

q: Is notice period mandatory?
a: Yes. Employees must follow the notice period mentioned in appointment terms or applicable policy unless waived by the authorized authority.

q: What happens if notice period is not served?
a: Notice pay adjustment may apply, subject to appointment terms, policy and management approval.

q: Is clearance required before final settlement?
a: Yes. Final settlement is processed after completion of required clearance from relevant departments.

q: When is final settlement paid?
a: Final settlement is paid after clearance, calculation, approval and payroll/finance processing as per company procedure.

q: What conduct is expected from employees?
a: Employees are expected to act professionally, ethically, respectfully and in the best interest of ACI.

q: Can employees share confidential company information?
a: No. Confidential company information must not be disclosed without authorization.

q: Can employees make public or media statements on behalf of ACI?
a: Employees should not make public, media or social media statements on behalf of ACI unless officially authorized.

q: Can employees accept gifts from suppliers or business partners?
a: Employees should avoid accepting gifts, benefits or favors that may create conflict of interest or influence business judgment.

q: What happens in case of misconduct?
a: Misconduct may lead to disciplinary action, including warning, suspension, termination, dismissal or other action as per policy and applicable law.
